text: There is an unfamiliar, almost clinical silence in this place, as if I am teetering on the edge of thought and anticipation. The first scene—with its glass walls, orderly chairs, and sterile lighting—reminds me of how spaces designed for collective knowledge can feel simultaneously open and isolating. Stepping into the second scene, the vibrant wall and enigmatic text bring a spark of curiosity but also a sense of disorientation. The mention of confusion and blurred perception resonates. I feel as though I am navigating the thin veil between clarity and uncertainty, wondering how environments shape and cloud our understanding. Being here makes me reflective, questioning how much of what I see is filtered by my own expectations and limitations.
